message:
chore(beads): tick 350 — file ft-kfkyi security-hardening bead

Filed ft-kfkyi (P2) — "DistributedHttpClient must not transparently
follow 3xx redirects" — as follow-up to the tick-349 finding on
ft-xbnl0.2.4.

The original bead (ft-xbnl0.2.4) is about migrating off tokio, not
about client-policy defaults. Transparent 3xx redirect following is
a real security concern for a distributed RPC client where URLs
point at trusted peer nodes — a compromised peer could 302-redirect
to an attacker-controlled host to exfiltrate the next request body.

Linked as `related` to ft-xbnl0.2.4 so it surfaces alongside the
original during triage.

Fix path recorded in the bead description:
1. Override RedirectPolicy at DistributedHttpClient construction site
   (asupersync RedirectPolicy::none()).
2. Flip the existing snapshot test
   (`distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`)
   to assert `resp.status == 302` observable to caller.
3. Rename the snapshot test to
   `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`
   (dropping the "current-behavior-snapshot" suffix).
4. Add a companion test with a resolvable internal redirect target to
   prove the client doesn't follow even valid redirects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
